notes: Third-generation CX300/CX500/CX700 modular FC disk arrays; 25-100% faster than predecessors; superseded by VNX then Unity/PowerStore
Entry-level FC meets Serial ATA; scales to 3TB on SATA; wizard-based setup; Dell-conceived product concept
Family of NAS servers (775N/770N/745N) using Windows Storage Server 2003; evolved into PowerVault NX series then PowerScale
Tape automation products: 122T autoloader; 132T/136T tape libraries; 160T enterprise library (LTO-2 only)
Primary SAN interconnect at 2Gb/s era; Dell participating in FCLC standards; still used at 32/64Gb/s
Lower-cost higher-capacity disk interface for midline storage; Dell participating in Serial ATA II standards development
Block-level storage networking; Dell|EMC CX arrays are SAN building blocks; Brocade switches and QLogic HBAs
File-level storage; Dell PowerVault NAS consolidates file servers; supports CIFS/AFP/NFS protocols
NAS OS based on Windows 2003 Advanced Server; used on all PowerVault NAS platforms; standards-based alternative to proprietary NAS OS
Device management software for Dell|EMC CX arrays; superseded by Unisphere for VNX/Unity
Point-in-time copy software for CX family; available across CX300 through CX700 and AX100; evolved into Dell EMC snapshot technologies
Open tape standard; Dell standardizing enterprise tape (PowerVault 160T) on LTO-2; current generation LTO-9 at 18TB native
